item.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

item.title: The climate tech IPO window could finally be cracking open
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Nuclear startup X-energy went public, geothermal startup Fervo is about to. Could this be the moment that climate tech investors have been waiting for?

item.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

item.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

item.title: Apps to distract you from the endless cycle of doomscrolling
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: It's hard to break the cycle of doomscrolling, but there are plenty of apps that can help you spend more time on content that’s engaging and productive.

item.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

item.title: India’s Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing one million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

item.title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Steve Ballmer blasts founder he backed who pleaded guilty to fraud: ‘I was duped and feel silly’
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Steve Ballmer wrote a fiery letter in the sentencing of disgraced founder Joseph Sanberg documenting all the harm that's befalling him as an investor.

item.title: Palantir is reportedly helping the IRS investigate financial crimes
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The IRS has used Palantir's software since at least 2018, The Intercept reports.

item.title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

item.title: Two college kids raise a $5.1 million pre-seed to build an AI social network in iMessage
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Series, a social networking app that's grown popular on college campuses, announced a $5.1 million pre-seed round from some big names in tech.

item.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

item.title: X-energy stock pops 27% on first day of trading following upsized IPO
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Investors flocked to nuclear power startup X-energy in its first day of public trading on the Nasdaq.

item.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

item.title: X launches stand-alone XChat app on iOS
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X's new app promises private chats, disappearing messages, audio and video calls, and more.

item.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

item.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

item.title: Nuclear startup X-energy raises $1B in data center-driven IPO
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Amazon-backed X-energy was able to raise around 20% more money in its IPO as demand for nuclear power surges.

item.title: Another spyware maker caught distributing fake Android snooping apps
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Researchers have found a new case where government authorities used a fake Android app to plant spyware on a target’s phone. The company that allegedly developed the spyware was not previously known to sell this type of software.
